questions.prompt: Your classifier is 68% accurate using only a description of the task. Best next move?

choices.text: Add 3–5 few-shot examples wrapped in <example>/<input>/<output> tags. Cover the input distribution and at least one edge case. This is the highest-ROI improvement for classification tasks — typically jumps 15–30 points without changing the model.
Add more adjectives to the description.

questions.explanation: Examples capture patterns descriptions can't. Three rules for good few-shot: cover the input distribution, include edge cases, show output exactly as you want it. Wrap each example in tags so Claude can parse the structure cleanly.

questions.prompt: Your stable example block is 1,500 tokens and you make 20,000 calls/day. What pattern keeps the quality without crushing the bill?

choices.text: Mark the example block as cacheable with `cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' }` in the system or user content. Cached input on Claude is ~10% the price of fresh input, so repeat calls within the cache window pay almost nothing for the example block.
Switch to a different model every other call.
Concatenate all 20,000 user inputs into one giant call.

questions.explanation: Prompt caching is the right pattern for stable example blocks. Module 5 covers caching in depth — the short version is: a one-line config change unlocks ~90% cost reduction on the cached portion when calls share a prefix.
